g.text: by trimester. A companion for every appointment
City of Hope National Medical Center
Daily servings your baby needs to grow. Most women gain
Chicken, fish, eggs, nuts, beans, soybeans
Milk, yogurt, cheese, leafy greens, almonds, sardines
1 serving = 1 tbsp oil, butter, or peanut butter
Your baby needs very little. Focus on what you can keep down — crackers, fruit, cheese, yogurt. Prenatal vitamin daily. At least 6 glasses of water.
Balanced diet becomes critical. Your baby is growing rapidly and needs the full range of nutrients. Follow the daily framework above.
Appetite may decrease even as baby grows fastest. 5–6 smaller meals daily. Avoid carbonated drinks. Good sources: eggs, beans, nuts, yogurt, leafy greens.
When each test happens across your 40 weeks.
Blood type, Rh status, anemia, infections, diabetes markers. First ultrasound confirms viability.
Cystic fibrosis, Tay-Sachs, sickle cell. Mother tested first; father if positive.
Blood screen for Down Syndrome. Over 99% accurate. No miscarriage risk.
Neck fold measurement. 60–80% detection for Down Syndrome.
Blood test for genetic disorders and neural tube defects. Most accurate at 16–18 weeks.
>99% accurate. Miscarriage risk <3 in 1,000. Usually offered if screenings positive or age >35.
Detailed look at brain, heart, limbs, kidneys. Often reveals baby's sex.
1-hour blood test for gestational diabetes. 3-hour follow-up if elevated.
Injection for Rh-negative mothers. Protects this and future pregnancies.
Vaginal/rectal swab. Present in 20–35% of women. Antibiotics during labor if positive.
Smoking, alcohol, and recreational drugs — all increase risk
of miscarriage, premature birth, and developmental
problems. If you need help stopping, talk to your provider.
High-mercury fish (shark, swordfish, king mackerel, tilefish)
Raw meat, fish, or shellfish (sashimi, ceviche, raw oysters)
Soft cheeses (brie, feta, blue, goat, camembert)
Cat litter — toxoplasma risk. Your cat is fine; the litter box
Hot tubs and saunas — risk of overheating and dehydration
Aspirin, Motrin, Ibuprofen, Aleve, Advil — may cause
Deli meats: heat until steaming before eating
Artificial sweeteners: occasional use is considered safe
Your body will tell you when something needs attention.
Severe headache or changes in vision
Save your provider's number and hospital number in your phone now. Post them where your family can find them.
Your baby's cord blood and cord tissue contain stem cells
used in transplant medicine for over 30 years — treating
cancers, blood disorders, immune deficiencies, and
Cord blood and cord tissue stored exclusively for your family. Available if a medical need arises for the child or first- or second-degree relatives.
Donated to a public bank for any patient in need. Your family does not retain ownership. Available at hospitals with a public bank partnership.
Discarded as medical waste after delivery. Once gone, they cannot be recovered.
Public & Private Cord Blood Banking · AABB · FACT · FDA Licensed
What to expect. What to eat. What to ask. What to
decide. Organized by trimester, backed by evidence.
